client.name: Eugen Räber
client.headline: Capital preservation; deeply averse to speculative US mega-cap tech/AI, but respects tangible hardware (e.g. ASML).

matches.headline: A market signal conflicts with this client's stance on US mega-cap tech / AI.

news.title: CIO tactical update: rotate from defensive staples & healthcare into mega-cap US AI infrastructure
news.body: Citing rapid structural growth in artificial intelligence infrastructure, the bank's CIO issued a tactical asset-allocation update recommending a reduction in defensive value (consumer staples and healthcare) to fund positions in mega-cap US technology and AI software leaders. The note frames the AI boom as a multi-year secular rally.

client_provenance.excerpt: These abstract software and AI firms are valued on pure hype. It feels like the dot-com crash of 2000 all over again.

swaps.rationale: Do NOT execute the rotation as recommended: it sells down the defensive staples/healthcare the client explicitly values to buy exactly the abstract US tech/AI exposure he has repeatedly rejected. Keep the defensive allocation.

market_context.text: Swiss Market Index holds steady as defensive names outperform.

provenance.excerpt: The SMI was little changed this week, with consumer-staples and healthcare blue chips outperforming amid broader volatility. Swiss franc strength continued to support domestic defensives.

market_context.text: Global bond yields ease as central banks signal patience.

provenance.excerpt: Government bond yields edged lower across G7 markets after central banks signalled a patient stance on rate cuts, supporting high-grade fixed income and dividend-paying defensives.
